subject: Partnership opportunity for Cleverly Me readers — music education
body:
Hi Ana,

I came across Cleverly Me while researching Miami mom blogs and loved your authentic voice on balancing family life, budget-conscious parenting, and creating memorable experiences with your kids. The way you've partnered with brands like Disney, Huggies, and South Florida Parenting shows you genuinely care about what you recommend to your readers.

I'm Keri from Thoven (thoven.com), a music education platform where families find credentialed music teachers for live private lessons. We work with teachers from top conservatories like Juilliard and Manhattan School of Music, and families can choose online or in-person lessons with integrated progress tracking.

I think Thoven could be a great fit for Cleverly Me readers who are looking for enriching activities for their kids. We've had success with similar partnerships — one mom influencer recently shared Thoven with her community and it resonated well.

Would you be open to a brief chat about how this might work for your audience?

Best,
Keri
Thoven
keri@thoven.co
personalized_notes: Referenced her brand partnerships (Disney, Huggies, South Florida Parenting) and budget-conscious parenting angle

subject: Partnership inquiry — Laura Scott & music education platform
body:
Hi Claudia,

I'm reaching out on behalf of Thoven (thoven.com), a music education platform that connects families with credentialed music teachers for live private lessons.

We've been following Laura's journey as a Harvard-trained dermatologist, mom of five, and creator behind A Little Bit of Lacquer and Laura Scott & Co. Her message about making the impossible look achievable really resonates — and we think Thoven could align well with her audience of ambitious moms balancing career and family.

Thoven offers live 1-on-1 music lessons (online or in-person) with teachers from conservatories like Juilliard and Manhattan School of Music, plus integrated progress tracking and parent communication.

Would Laura be open to exploring a partnership? We're flexible on structure and happy to discuss what would work best for her community.

Best,
Keri Erten
Co-founder, Thoven
keri@thoven.co
personalized_notes: Reached out to her manager at Digital Brand Architects. Highlighted her doctor/mom journey and achievement-oriented audience.

subject: Partnership opportunity — music lessons for busy families
body:
Hi Natalie,

I've been following At Home With Natalie and love how you make motherhood feel like coffee with a neighborhood friend — warm, real, and full of practical ideas. Your DIY party planning and kid-friendly content shows you understand what busy moms need.

I'm Keri from Thoven (thoven.com), a music education platform where families find credentialed music teachers for live private lessons. We work with teachers from top conservatories and offer both online and in-person options with built-in progress tracking.

I think Thoven could resonate with your readers — especially moms looking for enriching activities that fit into hectic schedules. We'd love to explore a partnership, whether that's through a lesson credit for your family to try, or another structure that works for you.

Would you be interested in learning more?

Best,
Keri
Thoven
keri@thoven.co
personalized_notes: Referenced her 'neighborhood friend' vibe and DIY/party planning content. Mentioned lesson credit model as an option.
